title: Data Analyst
description: The Ardonagh Analytics Lab, based in Mullingar, Ireland, delivers unrivalled risk insight and innovative data\-driven solutions for The Ardonagh Group’s collective of leading businesses, their customers and insurance partners. With more than 50 diverse brands worldwide, we have access to unique perspectives into emerging market trends and the solutions needed to meet customer needs on a global scale. At the core of our business lies a commitment to innovation. Working in areas such as Big Data, Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence our team of talented experts work at the very forefront of data analytics development, harnessing the latest technologies and tools to build our suite of ground\-breaking solutions. At the Ardonagh Analytics Lab we believe that data and technology should be used to enhance our people driven relationships – not replace them. We are a family of like\-minded, entrepreneurial individuals and we welcome people from all walks of life. Our shared values shape a unique culture where everyone can thrive. The Ardonagh Analytics Lab is part of The Ardonagh Group, the UK’s largest independent insurance distribution platform and a top 20 broker globally. Ardonagh is home to a set of best\-in\-class and specialist brands with a network of more 150 locations worldwide and a combined workforce of nearly 12,000 people. **Responsibilities** * Help turn data into information and information into insight. * Using a variety of tools and query techniques, work with and interpret a variety of data sets to develop the insight that solves problems and identifies business opportunities. * Act as the key link between the Data Engineering and Data Visualisation Teams to understand the data sources available, identify opportunities for its value \& use and ensure the insights that can be derived are available for presentation and access by colleagues and clients across The Group. * Communicate data\-driven results and recommendations clearly to technical and non\-technical audiences. * Supplement and enrich automated reporting solutions with the analytical insight that leads to action and improvements in business performance. * Help drive a culture change around the use and purpose of data to ensure actions are driven by fact based insight. * Help define, implement and lead on robust data management and reporting processes across The Group. * Identify and help standardise the use and governance of data. * Design and implement methods to ensure consistent application and use of analytics for the organisation. **Person Specification** * Min. 5 years data analysis \& reporting experience * Strong knowledge of SQL and experience in data querying, data mining, data segmentation. * Strong quantitative analysis skills and experience in developing calculations to interpret and assess data sets to produce insight. Excellent problem solving skills. * 2\+ years experience using statistical analysis languages (R, Python, etc.). * Good understanding of database concepts and understanding of structures required. * Strong understanding of Business Intelligence strategies/design as it relates to Data analysis. * Financial services and ideally General Insurance experience strongly desired. * Experienced and independent subject matter expert, capable of working on own initiative whilst delivering best practice solutions that deliver the strategic objectives. **The Company** In an industry that never stands still, neither do we. The Ardonagh Group was founded in 2017 and has grown to become the UK’s largest independent insurance distribution platform and a top 20 broker globally. Our best\-in\-class entrepreneurial and specialist brands span a network of 150\+ locations worldwide and have a combined workforce of more than c.10,000 people. Across our portfolio, we offer a highly diversified range of insurance\-related products and services across the full insurance value chain in the UK, Ireland and broader international markets. From complex multinational corporations to individuals purchasing personal insurance policies, our understanding of the communities we serve, together with our scale and breadth, allows us to deliver a broad range of product and risk solutions to meet the needs of a rapidly evolving market. If you approach life with a sense of adventure you might just find what you need at Ardonagh to bring your ambition to life. Find out more about Ardonagh’s world of opportunity on LinkedIn or visit **www.ardonagh.com** . \#AAL

title: Data Center Design Engineer
description: Crusoe is on a mission *to accelerate the abundance of energy and intelligence* . As the only vertically integrated AI infrastructure company built from the ground up, we own and operate each layer of the stack — from electrons to tokens — to power the world's most ambitious AI workloads. When you join Crusoe, you join a team that is building the future, faster. We're in the midst of the greatest industrial revolution of our time. The demand for AI compute is boundless, and power is a bottleneck. We're solving that — with an energy\-first approach that makes AI infrastructure better for the world and faster for the people innovating with AI. We're looking for problem\-solving, opportunity\-finding teammates with a sense of urgency, who believe in the scale of our ambition and thrive on a path not fully paved — people who want to grow their careers alongside a team of experts across energy, manufacturing, data center construction, and cloud services. If you want to do the most meaningful work of your career, help our customers and partners advance their AI strategies, and be part of a high\-performing team that believes in each other, come build with us at Crusoe. **About This Role:** As the Data Center Deployment Engineer, you will be the primary architect of Crusoe’s operational computing environments. You will bridge the gap between heavy infrastructure and high\-performance computing by designing the "whitespace"\- the critical area where our servers, storage, and network equipment live. Your work is fundamental to Crusoe’s mission of aligning the future of computing with the future of the climate; by optimizing power distribution, cooling, and equipment density, you directly enable the scalability and energy efficiency of our sustainable cloud platform. You will join a bold, collaborative team where you will lead the planning and implementation of high\-density layouts and modular infrastructure. The ideal candidate is a technical problem\-solver who thrives in the intersection of mechanical, electrical, and network engineering. This is a full\-time position designed for an innovative engineer who wants to build the physical foundation of an ethical and sustainable digital world. **What You’ll Be Working On:** * Whitespace Design \& Planning: Architect comprehensive whitespace layouts, including rack placement and aisle configurations, to maximize equipment density while maintaining optimal airflow and accessibility. * Infrastructure Integration: Partner with Electrical and Mechanical teams to seamlessly integrate power delivery and cooling systems into the whitespace, ensuring robust support for high\-performance cloud workloads. * Capacity \& Growth Modeling: Develop sophisticated 3D models and capacity planning tools to project future utilization, ensuring our data centers remain scalable and redundant (N\+1, 2N). * Connectivity Strategy: Coordinate with Network and Cloud teams to design and implement structured cabling and fiber management systems that meet rigorous performance and low\-latency requirements. * Cross\-Functional Leadership: Participate in design reviews and risk assessments, providing technical guidance to internal stakeholders and external contractors during construction and commissioning phases. * Compliance \& Excellence: Enforce industry standards (TIA\-942, ASHRAE) and local building codes to ensure every design is safe, compliant, and performs to the highest reliability standards. * Technical Documentation: Produce and maintain high\-fidelity CAD/BIM drawings and schematics that serve as the definitive source for operations, maintenance, and auditing. * Continuous Innovation: Evaluate emerging modular technologies and design methodologies to relentlessly improve energy efficiency and site performance. **What You’ll Bring to the Team:** * Engineering Foundation: A Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Mechanical, Facilities, or Data Center Engineering. * Industry Expertise: At least 5\+ years of experience in data center design, with a proven track record in cloud\-scale or hyperscale environments. * Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in CAD/BIM software (AutoCAD, Revit, Visio, or equivalent) to create precise architectural and engineering schematics. * Infrastructure Mastery: Deep technical knowledge of data center power distribution, UPS systems, HVAC/cooling systems, and structured cabling. * Standards Knowledge: A firm grasp of industry\-standard frameworks, specifically TIA\-942, Uptime Institute, ASHRAE, and ANSI/BICSI. * Collaborative Mindset: Exceptional communication skills and the ability to drive results within cross\-functional teams while managing multiple complex projects simultaneously. * Field Readiness: A willingness and ability to travel to data center sites to oversee implementation and conduct site inspections. **Bonus Points:** * Advanced Hyperscale Experience: Prior experience specifically designing for ultra\-high\-density liquid cooling or modular data center deployments. * Professional Certifications: Possession of a Registered Communications Distribution Designer (RCDD) or Certified Data Center Design Professional (CDCP) credential. * Sustainability Focus: Experience implementing LEED\-certified or carbon\-neutral data center cooling and power strategies. * Advanced Modeling Skills: Experience with Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) modeling to analyze and optimize airflow within the whitespace. * Leadership Tenure: Experience mentoring junior engineers or leading external vendor teams through complex infrastructure build\-outs. **Benefits:** Crusoe also offers a competitive benefits package designed to support financial security, health, and overall well\-being, including pension contributions, private health and dental insurance, income protection, life assurance and more. **Compensation:** Compensation will be paid as salary or hourly. Compensation to be determined by the applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data. Crusoe is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, citizenship, marital status, sex/gender, sexual preference/ orientation, gender identity, age, veteran status, national origin, or any other status protected by law or regulation.

title: Data Innovation Graduate Programme
description: Step into the heart of innovation with daa’s Data Innovation Graduate Programme. Over two years, you’ll rotate across our Data Analytics teams \- solving real\-world challenges that keep Ireland’s airports running smoothly. Whether you're building digital systems or turning data into insights, you’ll deliver impact across operations, retail \& commercial, digital, finance, and more. You will work with business teams to design data\-driven tools that interpret, model and predict across transactional, geographical, system event and other data to create models, apps and dashboards, that power smarter decisions. Ideal for inquisitive, analytical thinkers with strong communication skills and backgrounds in Computational Thinking , Maths / Stats, Economics, Physics, Engineering, or Product Design. This is a structured 2\-year graduate programme. As a Data Innovation Graduate, you’ll work at the intersection of data, insight, and business decision‑making. Across your rotations, you’ll help transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that support smarter choices across daa. Depending on your placement, you could be: * Analysing large and diverse datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities across airport operations, commercial and digital areas * Working with AI \& ML tools across cloud and on\-premise systems to accelerate \& transform how we use our data to solve business problems * Designing and delivering dashboards and reports that communicate insights clearly to business stakeholders * Working with teams to understand business questions, translating them into meaningful data analysis and measures * Supporting data‑driven decision‑making by evaluating performance, customer behaviour, and operational outcomes * Collaborating across multidisciplinary teams to ensure insights are understood, trusted, and used * Contributing to data products and analytical solutions that are scalable, well‑governed, and impactful At daa Labs, every day brings a new challenge—whether it's delivering cutting\-edge technology and data driven solutions to keep the airport running. The diversity of applications and data spans from airport operations, back\-office systems to commercial, digital and retail solutions, which makes this an uniquely dynamic and rewarding graduate opportunity. * **Not your average grad job** — it’s a launchpad for future tech leaders * **Hands\-on from day one** — build cloud infrastructure, apps, integrations and digital tools * **Real impact** — drive smarter decisions with data and innovation * **Data\-driven rotations** — design dashboards, build models, uncover insights * **Cross\-functional exposure** — work across cloud, cyber, BI, and more * **Innovation\-first culture** — your ideas matter and get implemented * **Build your portfolio** — solve real business problems, not just case studies * As a **Data Innovation Graduate** , you’ll work at the intersection of data, insight, and business decision‑making. Across your rotations, you’ll help transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that support smarter choices across daa. Depending on your placement, you could be: * **Analysing large and diverse datasets** to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities across airport operations, commercial and digital areas * **Working with AI \& ML tools** across cloud and on\-premise systems to accelerate \& transform how we use our data to solve business problems * **Designing and delivering dashboards and reports** that communicate insights clearly to business stakeholders * **Working with teams to understand business questions** , translating them into meaningful data analysis and measures * **Supporting data** ‑ **driven decision** ‑ **making** by evaluating performance, customer behaviour, and operational outcomes * **Collaborating across multidisciplinary teams** to ensure insights are understood, trusted, and used * **Contributing to data products and analytical solutions** that are scalable, well‑governed, and impactful After submitting your application, you’ll be invited to complete an online assessment. This must be completed within 5 days of receiving the invitation—applications without a completed assessment within this timeframe will not be considered **Do you have what it takes?** We’re looking for curious minds and bold thinkers ready to shape the future of airport technology. To thrive in this programme, you should: * **Hold (or be expecting) a 2\.1 degree or higher** in a relevant field such as Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Product Design, or related disciplines. * **Bring strong teamwork, communication, and interpersonal skills** — collaboration is at the heart of everything we do. * **Show ambition, integrity, and a growth mindset** — we want people who are eager to learn, adapt, and lead. * **Be resilient** — ready to take on challenges in a fast\-paced, ever\-evolving environment. **What We Are Offering You?** As a daa Data Innovation Graduate, you’ll enjoy a comprehensive package of benefits designed to support your growth, reward your performance, and set you up for long\-term success — both personally and professionally. From competitive compensation to world\-class development opportunities, you’ll be empowered to thrive in a collaborative, inclusive environment. **Programme benefits include:** * **Competitive salary** with a **performance\-related bonus** to reward your impact * **Flight subsidies and access to Fast Track** to help you stay connected and explore new destinations * **Support to earn Industry\-recognised certifications,** boosting your cloud skills and boosting your credibility in tech\-driven roles ( *Microsoft Azure Fundamentals, ITIL Foundation, Agile/SCRUM Master)* * **Structured learning and development** through tailored training, interactive workshops, hands\-on experience with Scrum and Project Management, technology platforms and engineering tools. * **Personalised coaching and mentoring** to guide your career journey * **Projects across** **daa’s** **business units** —including Airfield Operations, Airport Operations, Retail, and Commercial ecommerce often with direct placement in specialist teams * Exposure to **cutting\-edge technologies** in cloud and on\-premise tech including: MS Azure, Databricks, DevOps, GitHub, JIRA, Advanced ML, Oracle \& SQL Databases, Container applications and Enterprise systems * **A collaborative, inclusive culture** that values diversity, teamwork, and your unique contributions * **On\-site gym and pool** , plus social activities and volunteering opportunities to support your wellbeing and community engagement

title: Database Developer
description: **PFH Technology are currently seeking a .NET / Database Developer (Senior) in a hybrid capacity for our public sector client.** **Location: Flexible \- must be available to attend meetings at client sites as required** **Excellent Daily Rate – One year contract \+ Rolling Contract** **Background:** Within Technology and Transformation there is a requirement for a suitably skilled .Net / Database Developer with both coding and SQL / DBA responsibilities to join the Customer Service Experience (CSE) Service Operations Reporting team. The successful candidate will be required to support existing systems and processes while also providing strategic input in the areas of application and database design, performance optimization, business continuity and integration with other core systems. **Role:** The hybrid role will mostly revolve around maintenance and development of existing and green field .Net Applications with a secondary requirement to assist in developing and maintaining SQL Databases in an on\-prem and Azure environment. • Design cross platform / hybrid solutions to the highest standards using the best technological fit for the application • Engage stakeholders to identify specific requirements and bottlenecks, solve problems • Encourage the use of design principles **Relevant Experience** * 3\+ Year solution architecture and technical design experience of hybrid on\-prem and Cloud * platform applications. * Highly experienced C\# / Asp Developer (5\+ years) * ICT project delivery and implementation planning * Experience working with SQL Server * Experience in Cloud based technologies including * Token\-based Authentication * DevOps and source management techniques in the cloud. * Continuous Integration, Continuous Delivery, Azure DevOps etc. * Leading architecture using modern software engineering and product development tools * Experience designing and developing Web Applications using AngularJS, HTML, Web APIs * Ability to establish repeatable processes, best practices and a learning environment for junior developers * Systems integration and API development * Architecture and API integration * Software architecture and design * IT and information security * Software development and project lifecycle **Required Skills \& Competencies:** * C\#, SQL, MVC, Asp.Net * SQL Server, Oracle * IIS, RESTful APIs and Cloud infrastructure * Javascript, HTML, CSS * Azure, DevOps, CI\\CD. Pipelines and Source Control Toolsets - **Desirable Skills:** * MS PowerBI * ITSM experience, Ivanti Service Desk/Ivanti Neurons preferred. * Cloud Technologies: (Azure SQL, Data Factory, etc.) * Version Control: Familiarity with tools like Git for database scripts. * Automation tools like MS Power Automate. **Preferred Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related discipline. * Microsoft certifications (e.g. Microsoft Certified: Azure Database Administrator Associate or equivalent experience with SQL Server administration and development). **Data Governance \& Compliance:** * Experience with GDPR, data privacy, auditing in the public sector/health environment. **Personal Characteristics:** * Demonstrate good problem solving, analytical and decision making skills * The ability to interact in a professional manner with key stakeholders * Attention to detail and the ability to produce against set timelines * Self\-starter with a willingness to take responsibility * Excellent teamwork and interpersonal skills

title: Delivery Driver & Site Operative
description: Vista Roofing Ltd, a specialist roofing contractor in Parkwest, Dublin, seeks a reliable full\-time Multi\-Skilled Worker to support our operations. This hands\-on role combines driving, warehouse management, metal fabrication, and occasional work on construction sites to assist our teams when required. Competitive pay: €21\.37 per hour (39\-hour week, Mon\-Fri). Join our growing team delivering quality roofing solutions across the Greater Dublin Area. Key Responsibilities * Collect construction materials from suppliers and deliver them safely to various sites (CE license required). * Manage warehouse stock: receive goods, organise inventory, and maintain a clean, efficient space. * Operate press brake folding machines (manual) at our Dublin stores to fabricate basic metal pressings for use on Vista projects, training provided. * Deliver fabricated components directly to construction sites. * Assist roofing operatives on construction sites approximately 5\-7 days per month, depending on workloads. Requirements * Full CE or C license with clean driving record. * Strong time management, reliability, and teamwork; based in Parkwest, Dublin. * Construction/roofing background advantageous but not mandatory. * Manual handling and safe pass advantageous but not mandatory. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €42,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Position Overview DevOps Engineers are critical when it comes to keeping both our customers and engineering team running. As part of the DevOps group you will work on products critical to our operations and software that proactively detects issues with systems in the data center, in the cloud, or at customer sites. Responsibilities DevOps Engineers will have a variety of responsibilities which may include things like: * Design and implement pipelines and tools to ensure continuous integration, delivery, and deployment for our products * Define and implement tools that enable other teams to follow DevOps best practices * Own and deliver a suite of platform services consumed by internal stakeholders across teams and functions * Ensure production deployments are fast, reliable, and safe * Work with geo\-distributed development teams to improve quality and time\-to deploy * Automate, Automate, Automate Qualifications * Required * Relevant Bachelor's degree (Computer Science, Engineering, etc.) * 1\-2 years hands\-on DevOps experience * Belief in DevOps and Agile principles * Zealous about automation * Comfort in dealing with Cloud deployments, networking, and security best practices * CI/CD pipeline experience (Gitlab, Jenkins, etc.) * Linux system proficiency * Preferred * Strong container/serverless experience * Proficient knowledge of Python language * Hands on experience on Amazon Web Services (AWS) * Experience on Hashicorp ecosystem \- Terraform, Packer, Consul, Vault * Experience with telemetry tools/pipelines (metrics, logs, and traces)

title: DevOps Engineer, Charles River Development, Officer
description: We are looking for a DevOps engineer to join our growing Platform Engineering / DevOps Enablement team. Our Platform Engineering organization is modernizing the platform through cloud\-native technologies built on Microsoft Azure, Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, and modern CI/CD tooling. The DevOps team plays a critical role in this transformation. **Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and maintain CI/CD pipelines and supporting automation. * Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code for cloud environments. * Support Kubernetes\-based deployment workflows including Helm. * Use standard tooling such as (or similar to) GitHub, Azure DevOps, TeamCity, and JFrog Artifactory. * Support secure delivery practices including SSO, access management, and security scanning. * Troubleshoot and resolve build, pipeline, and environment issues. * Collaborate with engineers, architects, and security partners. * Contribute to operational documentation and runbooks. * Engage in agile ceremonies and global platform initiatives. **Qualifications** Recommended Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering or related field. **Recommended Experience** * 2\+ years of professional experience in DevOps or related engineering roles. * Hands\-on experience with CI/CD pipelines and automation concepts. * Hands\-on experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g. Terraform). * Familiarity with Azure services and Azure Portal usage. * Basic Kubernetes and container workflow experience. * Familiarity with artifact repositories and build tooling. * Awareness of secure delivery and documentation practices. * Experience leveraging AI tools as part of routine development activities. **Supervisory Responsibility** ☒ Individual Contributor ☐ Team Lead ☐ Manager of Managers Information Classification \*General **About State Street** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-788291

title: Driver/Warehouse Operative
description: Comprising over 77 Distribution Centres across 8 countries, Alliance Automotive Logistics stands as the pivotal distribution arm of Alliance Automotive Group and has rooted its reputation on the principles of service excellence, its mission to cultivate and nurture enduring partnerships with its extensive clientele and suppliers, and aiming to provide sustained value and reliability in all its operations. Specialising in providing an unparalleled range of logistical solutions and delivering over 250,000 parts to garages and repair centres throughout Europe, Alliance Automotive Logistics guarantee that garages and fast\-fit centres can repair and maintain their customers vehicles as quickly and efficiently as possible. Due to recent growth and expansion we are looking for Warehouse Operatives at our site based in Park West, Dublin 12\. **Responsibilities:** **Driver** * Pack and load products onto vans according to company’s specifications * Deliver orders to customers on assigned routes * Maintain ongoing communication with route dispatcher * Unload goods as per client specifications * Review deliveries with customers to ensure products meet their needs * Obtain delivery confirmations from each customer **Warehouse Operative** * Picking customer orders, loading vans and making regular checks to ensure all orders are picked correctly * Oversee the dispatch of orders and coordinate van deliveries * Checking and putting away supplier deliveries, informing your line manager of any shortages or damaged stock * Assist with a rolling stock taking program, ensuring all stock is located correctly at all times * Assist with maintaining a clean and safe working environment **Essential requirements;** * Flexible * Full Driving Licence * Good timekeeping Not the right fit? title: Dublin Cabin Crew Opportunities (Dubai Based, Relocation Provided)
description: **Job Purpose** A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew. As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you need to be friendly, observant and able to provide the right support. Being a member of the cabin crew is so much more than a service role \- safety is our highest priority. You’ll need to lead confidently and take control when it comes to managing aircraft services, security, and safety procedures. This comes from the world\-class learning experience our crew receive at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in Dubai. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Here are some other things we look for in our cabin crew:** * You’ve had more than a year’s experience in hospitality/customer service * You have a positive attitude and the natural ability to provide excellent service in a team environment, dealing with people from many cultures * Your minimum qualification is high school graduate (Grade 12\) * You need to be fluent in written and spoken English * You’re at least 160cm tall and can reach 212 cm while standing on tiptoes, to enable you to reach emergency equipment on all aircraft types * No visible tattoos while you’re in Emirates cabin crew uniform (without covering them with bandages or cosmetics) * As Emirates cabin crew, you’ll be based in Dubai and will need to meet the UAE's employment visa requirements Aside from the requirements of the role, you should be determined to always perform to the highest standards, focus on being solution oriented, and be able to manage a demanding work schedule. You should have the ability to deliver an authentic experience to our customers. You’ll be culturally aware and reflect the Emirates personality \- professional, empathetic, progressive, visionary and cosmopolitan. Before clicking the Apply button, please keep the following **documents** ready to submit with your application: * Recent CV in English * Recent photo **Salary \& Benefits** Your pay is made up of three components: 1. a fixed basic salary, 2. an hourly pay for operated flights and 3. an overseas meal allowance. Basic salary \= AED 4,980 / month, Flying Pay \= AED 69\.60 / hour based on avg. 80\-100 hours / month, Average Total Pay \= AED 11,244 / month (\~USD 3,100, EUR 2,600 or GBP 2,280\) \* These are approximate numbers for Grade II (Economy Class). Meal allowances for night stops are credited to the salary in arrears the following month. Hotel accommodation as well as transport to and from the airport is provided by the company.

title: ETF Trade & Fund Operations Analyst
description: **Job Description** Join our Team and play a crucial role in assisting clients to launch and service Exchange\-Traded Funds (ETFs). Our team provides an integrated suite of services including global custody, fund accounting and administration, transfer agency, and basket settlement services As an Exchange Trading Fund Operations Analyst within our comprehensive services team, you will be instrumental in helping clients mitigate risk, enhance returns, and increase efficiency. Leveraging our global resources. You will be involved in launching innovative fund structures and engaging our clients through a consultative process. **Job Responsibilities** * Help manage \& monitor the end to end ETF Lifecycle from order placement to settlement of burst transactions; Monitoring incoming cash receipts on ETF orders and managing incoming/outgoing collateral on ETF orders. * Complete Portfolio Composition File (PCF) Analysis and Trouble\-shooting. * Research and resolve client inquiries regarding trade and settlement activities in a timely manner. * Build relationships with J.P. Morgan’s Global ETF Clients, Authorized Participants and External Vendors and maintain an excellent and interactive relationship with internal partners including Client Services, Custody, Fund Accounting \& Global ETF Team. * Drive efficiencies and process improvements within the team and oversee management reporting and efficiency metrics. * Support new business initiatives \& Authorized Participant on\-boards. * Work closely with Product development and Technology to ensure successful development and implementation of new products and product enhancements. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills** * 1\+yrs industry experience * Excellent analytical \& problem solving skills. * Ability to complete multiple tasks accurately and in a timely manner. * A strong team player with an ability to work in teams distributed across the globe, ability to influence across multiple teams. * Adaptability to a fast paced, consistently changing environment. * Strong written and verbal communication skills with complimentary presentation skills **About Us** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **About The Team** J.P. Morgan’s Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: Electrical Day Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Day Technician** **Contract Duration/Type:** **Permanent** **Location: Clondalki, Dublin** **Hours:** **Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30** **Overview of the role:** The Lead Technicians will train on all Processes and techniques across Predictive Maintenance, CMMS (Pemac), Reliability techniques,HV and LV distribution, Turbines, Boilers Compressors, Diesel Generators Pumps, The working hours are Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30 The day lead technician may be required from time to time to back fill on shift, and may also be required to support the site in emergency situations outside of normal working hours. **Duties of the Role include:** * Coordinate the day to day work with shift technicians on\-site particularly morning shift changeover * Be the point of contact for service providers * Trouble\-shooting and high\-level investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures – root cause analysis * Compliance/quality engineering – recording and investigation of deviations on utility plant and equipment * Carry out planned preventive, predictive and corrective maintenance as required * Ensure PMs are completed on time * Good knowledge and understanding of CMMS systems * Develop PMs and Job Plans as well as SOPs for plant equipment. * Liaising with site planner re stores to identify spare part requirements; * Review maintenance programme periodically to ensure optimal uptime levels are achieved * Support and comply with all Safety procedures * Experience with: HV and LV distribution. Electrical generation turbines, diesel generators, boilers and Fuel Oil storage and distribution systems. Industrial gas installations * Be able to take a leadership role in decision\-making as regards to plant operation and equipment suitability for operation; * Have sufficient knowledge of the plant to allow decision making in terms of taking plant off line, communicating with relevant people on site and be capable of identifying the action required to get plant back on line or introduce back\-up and standby systems; * Understand the actions to be taken in the event of a serious service outage; * Provide leadership and mentoring to the team in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Provide leadership and mentoring to team members in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Support the Site engineer \& HV Engineer in carrying out Root Cause Analysis on plant failures to determine the sequence of events, the underlying root cause and assist the in developing proposals to remedy the fault; * Provide support to capital improvement projects on utilities systems, and be involved in the commissioning and hand over from projects to operations. * Assist the Site Engineer \& HV Engineer in planning and executing complex utility service isolations for maintenance, projects and client works; * Identify, develop and, if approved, implement Continuous Improvement measures to improve the efficiency of plant and working practices; * Seek to continuously improve knowledge and understanding of complex mechanical systems; **Required skills for this role:** * Analytical mindset * Experience with six sigma/lean preferred * Previous experience required, craft qualified electrician * Past working experience ideally gained in energy conversion and distribution **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Engineering Graduate 2026
description: Location: Leixlip, Ireland Category: Engineering Job ID: 21921 Description **Discover your exciting role** ------------------------------- We are seeking highly motivated **Engineering Graduates** to join our talented team at Exyte. As an Engineering Graduate, you will have the opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and gain hands\-on experience in delivering cutting\-edge projects. You will play an integral role in various stages of project design, development, and implementation, while contributing to the creation of state\-of\-the\-art facilities. For our 2026 Graduate Programme, we are recruiting for the following **engineering** disciplines: * Mechanical * Electrical * BIM/Design * Civil/Structural * Architectural * Process * Quality * Commissioning **Explore your tasks and responsibilities** ------------------------------------------- * Assist in the design, planning, and execution of engineering projects within our sectors of expertise. * Support senior engineers in preparing technical documentation, drawings, and specifications. * Participate in site visits and contribute to project assessments and feasibility studies. * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure seamless integration of engineering systems. * Develop and maintain project schedules, budgets, and risk management plans. * Learn and apply industry standards, safety regulations, and best practices to all tasks. * Continuously expand your knowledge of engineering processes and systems, with mentorship and guidance from experienced engineers. **Show your expertise** ----------------------- **Who You Are:** * A final year student or recent graduate with a relevant engineering degree. * Passionate about engineering and technology with a strong desire to build a successful career in the industry. * Excellent problem\-solving skills, with a keen eye for detail and the ability to think critically. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to collaborate effectively in a team environment. * Able to prioritize tasks, work independently, and meet deadlines. * Enthusiastic, adaptable, and eager to learn in a fast\-paced, innovative environment. **What We Offer:** * Competitive salary and benefits package, including healthcare \& pension. * Travel \& relocation opportunities across mainland Europe. * A dynamic and supportive work environment with opportunities for professional growth. * Exposure to innovative projects and advanced engineering technologies. * Mentorship and training programs designed to help you develop your technical and leadership skills. * A chance to work with some of the brightest minds in the industry.

title: Enterprise Risk Analyst (Aviation Leasing)
description: **Enterprise Risk Analyst (Aviation Leasing)** Our client is a global leader in aviation finance, renowned for connecting capital with customers to transform aviation and deliver the economic and social benefits of global travel. This role offers the opportunity to work in a dynamic and fast\-paced environment as an Enterprise Risk Analyst. Based in Dublin City Centre, this fixed\-term 12 month contract (maternity cover) offers hybrid working arrangements (4 days in the office and 1 day WFH). The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in supporting the Enterprise Risk Team, ensuring robust risk management and compliance processes while collaborating across various business functions. **Responsibilities** * Ensure compliance with shareholder reporting obligations, including coordinating audits and developing annual controls testing strategies * Continuously evaluate, update, and optimise controls, recommending automation where appropriate * Coordinate the controls testing process with internal and external auditors * Support the internal audit process by providing insights, facilitating questions, and advising on process improvements and risks * Manage Governance, Risk, and Compliance systems while leading specific initiatives that impact cross\-functional business areas **Requirements** * Third\-level education in Finance, Economics, or a related field * ACA/ACCA or equivalent qualification preferred * Minimum 5 years' of post\-qualification experience * Strong knowledge of US GAAP, IFRS, risk and controls standards, and business process best practices * Proven ability to identify and implement efficiencies and enhancements in processes and controls. * Good organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills * Ability to manage complex projects and deliver high\-quality results * Excellent interpersonal and collaborative skills

title: Financial Controller
description: **Suretank Limited** Suretank Group has been one of the leading Off\-Site Manufacturing Organisation (OSM) for over 30 years, offering solutions into the Offshore sector to include Tanks \& Specialist Containers and Modular Electrical and Plantroom Solutions into the Data Centre and Life science Sectors. Our target sectors include the Energy, Marine, Infrastructure \& Industrial sectors. Established in 1995, Suretank Group operates from its headquarters in Ireland, with additional manufacturing facilities in the UK, USA, Poland and other global locations. As we continue to expand, we are committed to delivering excellence and innovation while fostering a culture of collaboration and growth. **Job Summary:** The Financial Controller is responsible for the integrity of financial reporting, strengthening financial control, improving cash visibility, and supporting operational performance through disciplined financial management. The role combines technical accounting, working capital management, ERP discipline, operational finance support, and continuous improvement. **Key Responsibilities:** **Financial Control and Reporting** * Ensure integrity and accuracy of financial records * Lead monthly close and timely production of management accounts * Deliver variance analysis, margin reporting and performance insight * Maintain strong general ledger and balance sheet controls * Drive improvements in reporting processes **Cash and Working Capital** * Support and maintain short\-term cashflow forecasting * Improve visibility over receivables, payables and working capital movements * Support billing discipline, collections focus and cash conversion improvements * Escalate material cash risks and timing issues **Revenue, WIP and Operational Finance** * Strengthen controls around revenue recognition * Support WIP monitoring and project margin analysis * Reconcile operational activity to financial outcomes * Support improved linkage between production, billing and reporting **Budgeting and Forecasting** * Support annual budget preparation and periodic reforecasts * Maintain robust forecast assumptions and risk visibility * Support alignment of financial forecasts with operational realities **Inventory and Balance Sheet Control** * Support inventory valuation, controls and reconciliations * Strengthen inventory aging and provisioning discipline * Maintain strong control over key balance sheet accounts **Compliance and Audit** * Lead preparation and coordination of annual statutory financial statements * Manage year\-end close process to support timely completion of statutory accounts * Liaise with external auditors and coordinate all audit deliverables * Ensure audit readiness through strong controls, reconciliations and supporting documentation * Support timely filing of statutory returns and Companies Registration Office compliance **Tax, VAT and Compliance** * Support management of corporation tax, VAT compliance, RCT (where applicable), and broader tax compliance obligations * Support tax filings, Revenue interactions, audits and external adviser coordination * Maintain strong compliance discipline across statutory, regulatory and internal policy requirements **Treasury and Banking** * Support management of banking relationships and day\-to\-day treasury activities * Support liquidity planning, payment prioritisation and facility monitoring * Monitor covenant compliance where applicable * Support management of foreign exchange exposures where relevant * Support coordination of insurance renewals and related financial data requirements **Fixed Assets and Capex Control** * Maintain integrity of the fixed asset register * Ensure appropriate capitalisation, depreciation and asset controls * Support capex approval tracking and reporting * Support discipline around capital versus operating expenditure classification **Costing and Manufacturing Finance** * Support product, project and standard costing integrity * Support analysis of labour, overhead and material variances * Support stronger costing discipline and margin visibility **Internal Controls and Financial Policies** * Support development and enforcement of financial controls, approval matrices and financial procedures * Support periodic review and strengthening of internal controls * Identify, assess and escalate material financial and control risks, and support mitigation actions **Payroll Oversight** * Support review and oversight of payroll processes and controls * Review payroll reconciliations and related statutory deductions * Support payroll accuracy and compliance **Procurement Controls** * Support purchase order discipline, three\-way match controls and reduction of process leakage * Work with procurement and operations to improve control discipline **ERP and Systems** * Support integrity and effective use of SAP Business One * Drive improved data quality and system\-based reporting discipline * Support development of dashboards, exception reporting and improved management information **Business Partnering** * Work closely with operational stakeholders * Challenge assumptions where appropriate * Support stronger decision\-making through relevant financial insight **Cross\-Functional Support** * Provide proactive financial support across Operations, Supply Chain, Commercial, Customer Service and Project Delivery functions * Support commercial reviews, pricing support, margin analysis and contract/commercial input where required * Work collaboratively with the CSO/commercial function on forecasting inputs, revenue quality, pipeline assumptions and commercial discipline * Support broader business initiatives and transformation projects requiring financial and operational input **Team Leadership, Mentoring and Development** * Lead, mentor and develop finance team capability and bench strength * Coach junior and developing team members in technical competence, controls discipline and professional standards * Support succession planning, delegation and capability\-building across the finance function * Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement and cross\-functional problem\-solving removing process bottlenecks.Work closely with group finance and ERP / BI support resources **Essential** **Skills \& Experience Required** * Qualified ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent * 5\-8\+ years relevant post\-qualified experience * Experience in manufacturing, engineering or project\-based environments * Strong technical accounting and financial control capability * Experience in cashflow, forecasting and working capital management * ERP experience; SAP Business One desirable **Desirable** * Experience preparing or managing statutory financial statements * Experience managing external audits and year\-end processes * Experience in turnaround, growth or change environments * Exposure to BI reporting and data\-driven management information * Familiarity with project accounting and margin analysis **Personal Attributes** * Strong leadership, mentoring, and people development * Effective crossfunctional collaboration and business partnering * Solid financial and technical competence, including costing and manufacturing finance * Awareness of treasury, liquidity, internal controls, tax, VAT, and compliance * Strong analytical capability and commercial judgement * Continuous improvement mindset with strong process discipline * Excellent stakeholder management, planning, and organisational skills * High level of ownership, accountability, resilience, and adaptability **Performance Measures** * Success in role will include: * Timely, accurate monthly close * Improved cash visibility and forecasting reliability * Stronger balance sheet and control discipline * Better quality management information * Improved support for operational decision\-making * Continuous improvement in systems, controls and reporting **Company Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Company Pension Scheme * Company Healthcare Plan * Health and wellness support (e.g., EAP, mental health resources) * Death in Service Life Insurance * Long Term Illness Benefit * Free On\-site parking at all Suretank facilities * Training and development opportunities * Career progression within a growing company * Hybrid/Flexible working arrangements (where applicable) * Employee referral scheme

title: Financial Risk Analyst
description: **Role Overview** The Financial Risk Analyst supports the Chief Risk Officer in analysing and monitoring the company’s financial risk exposures and capital position. The role focuses on quantitative analysis of key insurance risks including underwriting, reserving, market and capital risks, while supporting regulatory risk management processes such as the Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA). Working closely with the Actuarial, Finance and Risk teams, the role contributes to financial risk monitoring, stress testing and risk reporting to ensure the organisation maintains a clear understanding of its financial risk profile. This role is well suited to an actuarial analyst or actuarial student seeking to develop experience in risk management within a regulated insurance environment. **Key Responsibilities:** **Financial Risk Monitoring** * Support the analysis and monitoring of financial risks including underwriting, reserving, market and capital risks. * Assist with monitoring solvency and capital metrics and analysing changes in the company’s risk profile. * Support the monitoring of financial risk indicators and risk appetite metrics. **ORSA Support** * Support the coordination and delivery of the Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA). * Assist with the preparation and analysis of stress and scenario testing. * Contribute to the preparation of sections of the ORSA report and supporting documentation. **Risk Monitoring and Reporting** * Assist with the preparation of financial risk reporting for senior management and the Board Risk Committee. * Analyse risk data and support the development of dashboards and risk reporting materials. **Enterprise Risk Management Support** * Support the operation of the Enterprise Risk Management framework. * Assist with updates to the risk register and risk monitoring processes where financial risks are involved. * Support the review and maintenance of risk management policies and documentation. **Risk Projects and Initiatives** * Support risk\-related projects and initiatives, including ESG and regulatory developments. * Contribute to Group risk initiatives and reporting where required. **Qualifications:** * Degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance or a related quantitative discipline. * Progress toward actuarial qualification (specifically a CERA qualification) preferred. **Skills:** * Strong quantitative and analytical skills. * Ability to analyse financial and actuarial data and translate findings into clear insights. * Strong Excel and data analysis capability. * Good written communication skills. * Ability to work collaboratively across teams including actuarial, finance and risk.

title: Financial Services Partner - FATCA & CRS & AEOI
description: Director / Senior Manager – Global Information Reporting (FATCA / CRS / AEOI) Big 4 Firm \| Hybrid \| Nationwide (Ireland) International tax transparency keeps evolving — and clients need advisers who can turn complex rules into practical, defensible reporting. Our client's Global Information Reporting / AEOI team supports leading financial services and corporate clients with FATCA, CRS and broader AEOI compliance, governance, and strategy. If you’re comfortable leading high\-profile engagements, shaping governance frameworks, and being the person clients ring first when deadlines or regulations shift, this is built for you. What you’ll be doing * Advising clients on FATCA/CRS obligations and compliance strategy * Leading end\-to\-end FATCA/CRS reporting and due diligence projects (including data interpretation and validation) * Designing and reviewing compliance frameworks, readiness assessments, and governance / risk controls * Supporting audit queries and regulatory interactions with clear, technical responses * Producing technical updates, client communications, and contributing to business development * Coaching junior colleagues and driving internal knowledge\-sharing What success looks like in your first 6–12 months * Become a trusted primary contact across a portfolio for FATCA/CRS matters * Deliver timely, accurate filings and well\-managed client projects * Strengthen client programmes through practical governance and control enhancements * Track regulatory developments and contribute to training and thought leadership What you’ll bring * Professional qualification: ACA / CTA (AITI) / equivalent, plus a relevant degree (Accounting/Finance/Law or similar) * Strong experience in tax advisory or financial services, with solid exposure to FATCA/CRS/AEOI * Strong technical grasp of Irish Revenue and OECD reporting requirements and how to apply them in practice * Proven ability to lead projects, manage stakeholders, and build long\-term client relationships * Tax technology / automation experience is a plus Location \& working model This role can be based in any Irish office, with a hybrid working model balancing client needs, office collaboration and flexibility. What you’ll get A competitive package plus strong investment in development: learning \& training, coaching, wellbeing supports, pension and enhanced leave — in an inclusive culture that expects high standards and backs you to grow.

title: Fleet
description: **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Responsibilities of the role:** Deliver best in class performance through professional, competitive and efficient buying processes, controls and systems for their project(s). Potentially leading and mentoring junior procurement personnel. * Develop and implement fleet, tool, and plant procurement strategies covering purchase, hire, lease, maintenance, and disposal. * Provide leadership and direction to FTP buyers and lead the strategic purchase and hire of fleet, plant, and tools to optimise utilisation and total cost of ownership. * Lead supplier sourcing, tendering, and commercial negotiations, including framework agreements, rebates, and strategic alliances. * Ensure the quality, compliance, and timely delivery of fleet tool and plant assets in line with project and operational requirements. * Conduct bid evaluations, commercial analysis, and approval recommendations for fleet and plant procurement activity. * Create and maintain fleet, tool \& plant procurement plans aligned with project teams and operational demand. * Monitor and report on fleet tool \& plant spend, utilisation, savings, supplier performance, and compliance. * Drive asset reallocation and recovery across projects to minimise external hire and excess spend. * Oversee the disposal, resale, or write‑off of surplus or end‑of‑life assets in line with policy and financial controls. * Ensure strict adherence to the Authority Matrix, procurement policies, and audit requirements. * Build strong relationships with project teams, site management, plant departments, finance, and senior stakeholders. * Conduct site visits to meet with project teams. * Significant European travel required. * Perform other duties as assigned by Line Manager **Essential Criteria of the Role:** * Min 5 years’ procurement experience, with strong fleet, tool and plant category expertise. * Experience in a similar role within an engineering or construction company. * Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. * Work well as part of a team towards a common goal. * Negotiating experience **Desirable Criteria:** * A third level qualification preferably in supply chain or other related discipline. * Previous SAP experience. * Previous experience leading a team * Knowledge of Tool and plant for construction an advantage. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer***

Proficient in .NET for backend development and React with TypeScript. Onsite work 2/3 days a week. REACT/ Typescript * Thrives in Agile environments, contributing effectively to sprint planning, daily stand\-ups, and retrospectives. Experienced in using Agile project management tools such as JIRA and Rally to track progress, manage tasks, and collaborate across cross\-functional teams. * Recognized for strong communication and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work closely with product owners, designers, QA engineers, and fellow developers to deliver high\-quality software that meets business requirements. Adept at translating complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Committed to clean code practices, continuous learning, and fostering a positive team culture. Passionate about solving challenging problems and delivering impactful solutions that drive user satisfaction and business success. title: GTM Strategy/Operations Associate - Dublin
description: Datadog's EMEA GTM Strategy \& Operations team is responsible for both strategic planning and day‑to‑day operational excellence across the region. The team uncovers, analyzes, and prioritizes improvement opportunities that drive incremental revenue and operational efficiency. We build business cases for EMEA investment, optimize resources during annual planning, and collaborate closely with global teams to enhance systems, tools, programs, and processes. We also lead the execution of global initiatives within EMEA and share best practices across regions to accelerate global scale. As part of the EMEA GTM Strategy \& Operations team, you'll work with sales leaders and global partners to improve how we plan, execute, and automate core GTM processes across the region. You'll be AI‑native in how you work, using modern AI tools to get to insight faster, diagnose root causes, and free your time for driving change and partnering with the business. *At Datadog, we place value in our office culture – the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work‑life harmony that best fits them.* **What You'll Do** * Build strong relationships with local, regional, and global cross‑functional stakeholders to enhance GTM strategy and operations for EMEA, sharing learnings and best practices across markets. * Localize and execute global GTM policies and sales programs (for example: pipeline generation programs, product plays, territory deep dives), ensuring they are adapted effectively to EMEA market realities. * Act as a primary point of contact for a defined set of sales leaders on strategic and operational questions (e.g. account coverage, rules of engagement, data led operating cadence, QBR inputs etc..). * Orchestrate and support the rhythm of business, including data preparation and standardization for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), forecast/LQSR cadences, and annual planning cycles. * Partner with systems, data, and central operations teams to simplify and automate recurring work (for example: standard QBR/LQSR kits, self‑serve lists and dashboards, queue and SLA reporting), using AI‑enabled workflows where it meaningfully reduces manual effort. * Produce analyses, reports, and recommendations on sales performance, coverage, pipeline, and program effectiveness, leveraging AI tools to explore data faster, spot patterns, and get to root causes so you can focus more time on driving action with stakeholders. * Manage cross‑functional projects from initiation to completion, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives, clear timelines, and well‑defined success metrics. * Document processes, definitions, and best practices so that outputs are scalable, repeatable, and easy for sales leaders and partner teams to adopt. Who You Are * 2–4\+ years of experience in GTM Strategy \& Operations, management consulting, banking, analytics, or a similar analytical field, ideally within SaaS or high‑growth B2B technology. * Experience working with go‑to‑market teams (e.g. Sales, CS, Marketing) and comfort acting as a trusted advisor to commercial leadership. * Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve business performance. * Comfort using modern AI tools as part of your day‑to‑day workflow (for example, to accelerate analysis, draft and refine narratives, or prototype simple automations), and curiosity to experiment with new capabilities. * Familiarity with Salesforce and BI tools (e.g. Tableau, Metabase, or similar); experience with SQL or a willingness to learn on the job. * High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast‑moving environment. * Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shifting between big‑picture strategic thinking and hands‑on execution. * Effective verbal and written communicator who can build alignment and influence cross‑functional partners. \#LI\-NG2 **About Datadog:** Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. title: Graduate Engineer - Water
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Company** At Nicholas O'Dwyer, sustainability is at the core of our work. If you are looking to advance your career in a socially responsible company, which has a proven track record over 90 years, and is committed to creating a sustainable planet, we invite you to be part of our team. Our mission is to provide innovative and reliable solutions using cutting edge technology and expertise, to ensure project success and employee satisfaction. We deliver infrastructure projects in water, wastewater, transportation, civil and structural engineering, flood protection, and energy including renewables. With headquarters in Ireland, we also operate in the UK, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. As part of the RSK Group, an amalgamation of over 200 companies with more than 17,000 employees, we benefit from a global network of opportunities while retaining our brand and autonomy. **About The Role** We are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic **Graduate Engineer** to join our Water Engineering Team. This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate to kick\-start their career in civil or environmental engineering, working on a diverse range of water infrastructure projects. As part of the team, you will gain invaluable hands\-on experience, develop your technical and project management skills, and work on projects that positively impact communities and the environment. You will have the opportunity to contribute to all phases of water engineering projects, from feasibility studies to detailed design and construction, while learning from experienced engineers in a collaborative and supportive environment. You will structured training and mentoring throughout your first year. In addition to office\-based learning and development, you will have practical exposure through site visits and involvement in site\-based activities. **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Building your knowledge of water engineering principles, regulations, and best practices. * Preparation of Feasibility, Route, and Site Selection Reports. * Contributing to the preparation of Concept and Detailed Design Reports, including drawings, calculations, and specifications. * Clean water network modelling with various modelling software in design and analysis. * Contributing to the preparation of Technical Specifications and Contract Documents for work and investigations. * Assisting in the preparation of documentation for statutory approvals, such as planning permissions and wayleaves. * Collaborate with senior engineers and project managers during construction stage contract administration. * Gaining exposure to developing detailed project plans, including budgets, schedules, and resource allocation. * Assisting in ensuring projects are delivered within budget, on time, and in compliance with regulations and standards. * Learning to identify and mitigate project risks under guidance from senior team members. * Fostering effective communication and collaboration within the team, clients, and stakeholders. * Ensuring all work adheres to Health \& Safety legislation and quality management systems. * Undertake additional responsibilities as required, in line with the evolving needs of the role and the business. * Opportunities to visit sites across Ireland/UK. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Civil or Environmental Engineering or a related discipline (or currently completing). * Interest in and basic understanding of water engineering principles and practices. * Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to work effectively as part of a collaborative team and build positive working relationships. * Methodical, detail\-oriented approach to problem\-solving, with curiosity, a willingness to ask questions, and an eagerness to learn through mentorship. * Self\-motivated and dependable, able to take initiative on assigned tasks while contributing positively within a supportive team environment. * Familiarity with design software such as AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GIS (ArcGIS \& QGIS) or similar tools is advantageous but not essential. * Familiarity with hydraulic modelling software such as Epanet, Synergi, MicroDrainage, HEC\-RAS or similar tools is advantageous but not essential. * Possession of a valid Irish driving license would be advantageous for site visits and project\-related travel. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?
